# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: my-public-api
  labels:
    app: my-app
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 80
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
    - port: 443
      targetPort: 443
      protocol: TCP
      name: https
  selector:
    app: my-app
  externalTrafficPolicy: Local
  sessionAffinity: None

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: my-app-container
          image: my-app-image:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 80
            - containerPort: 443
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 1000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL
